name: Beef Potpie
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the dough by beating the egg and adding milk until combined.
2. Gradually add flour to create a stiff dough.
3. Roll out the dough paper-thin and cut into 1-inch squares.
4. Keep the broth boiling while adding the dough squares, one at a time, to prevent them from sticking together.
Alternate Dough Preparation:
1. Add 4-5 tablespoons of broth to the flour and mix until it forms a stiff dough.
2. Roll out the dough paper-thin and cut into 1-inch squares.
3. Keep the broth boiling while adding the dough squares one at a time to prevent them from sticking together.
Final Preparation:
1. Cook the beef in salt water until tender (approximately 4 hours on low heat).
2. Remove the meat from the broth and add minced onions and parsley to the broth.
3. Bring to a fast boil and add alternate layers of cubed potatoes and squares of dough.
4. Cover and cook for 20 minutes, adding additional water if needed.
5. Add the cooked beef and stir through the potpie.
